title: Role-based login screen with per-role menu isolation

requirements.text: Create a login landing page (inspired by https://edumanage-9ftdqajs.manus.space/login/teacher) that presents four role-based login entry points: Student, Teacher, Institute, and Parents. Each role card/button should navigate to a dedicated login route (e.g., /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, /login/parent). The visual design should include a role selector with distinct icons and role-appropriate accent colors consistent with the existing per-role design tokens already defined in index.css.

quotes: can you add startinG like this

acceptanceCriteria: A landing page is shown at the root route (/) with four role login options: Student, Teacher, Institute, Parents.
Each role option links to its own login route.
Visual style references the reference URL design with role cards or segmented tabs.
Role accent colors use existing CSS custom property tokens (student, faculty, institute, parent).

requirements.text: Implement per-role login pages at /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, and /login/parent. Each page shows a login form (username/password fields with a Submit button — no backend authentication required, use mock/static credentials or simply a single 'Enter as [Role]' button). On successful login, store the active role in React state or context and redirect to the main app.

quotes: if student login does the student regarding all menu will come
teacher login menu happend then teacher regarding same for institute and parents

acceptanceCriteria: Visiting /login/student shows a Student-themed login form.
Visiting /login/teacher shows a Teacher-themed login form.
Visiting /login/institute shows an Institute-themed login form.
Visiting /login/parent shows a Parent-themed login form.
Clicking login/enter on each page stores the selected role and redirects to the dashboard.

requirements.text: After login, render only the menu and dashboard that belongs to the authenticated role. Student login → show only the Student dashboard and its sub-navigation (Academic Roadmaps, Exam Hub, Goals, Teams, Marketplace, Feedback). Teacher login → show only the Faculty Hub and its sub-navigation. Institute login → show only the Institute Command Center and its sub-navigation. Parents login → show only the Parent Insight Portal and its sub-navigation. All other role tabs must be hidden from the Navbar.

quotes: if student login does the student regarding all menu will come, teacher login menu happend then teacher regarding same for institute and parents

acceptanceCriteria: When logged in as Student, the Navbar shows only the Student tab and its sub-sections; Faculty Hub, Command Center, and Parent Portal tabs are not visible.
When logged in as Teacher, only Faculty Hub and its sub-sections are shown.
When logged in as Institute, only the Institute Command Center and its sub-sections are shown.
When logged in as Parents, only the Parent Insight Portal and its sub-sections are shown.
A logout button is visible on the Navbar that returns the user to the role selection landing page and clears the active role.

requirements.text: Add a role-aware auth context (e.g., AuthContext) that stores the currently logged-in role ('student' | 'teacher' | 'institute' | 'parent' | null). This context should be consumed by the Navbar to conditionally render tabs, and by the App router to guard routes — unauthenticated users are redirected to the login landing page.

quotes: if student login does the student regarding all menu will come, teacher login menu happend then teacher regarding same for institute and parents

acceptanceCriteria: AuthContext exposes currentRole and login(role) / logout() functions.
Navigating to / or any dashboard route while unauthenticated redirects to the role selection page.
Role persists across page refreshes (e.g., via sessionStorage or localStorage).
Logging out clears the role and redirects to the landing page.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts) or frontend/src/main.tsx.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui/.
Reuse all existing role-based CSS custom property design tokens from frontend/src/index.css — do not invent new color systems.
All existing dashboard components (StudentDashboard, FacultyIntelligenceHub, InstituteCommandCenter, ParentInsightPortal) must remain intact and continue to function correctly.
nonGoals: No real backend authentication — mock/static role login only.
No Internet Identity integration for this feature.
No changes to any existing dashboard sub-section components.
No multi-user account management or user profiles.

required: EduManage campus portal logo mark — a modern graduation cap combined with a circuit/network node motif, flat vector style with clean lines (edumanage-logo.dim_256x256.png)
